abstract.text: Make your photo and video app’s actions available to Apple Intelligence and Siri by adopting schemas for common photo and video actions.

inlineContent.text: The
domain defines app schemas that provide a
structured representation for common photo and video actions and content.
Apply schemas in the
domain to make your app’s photo and video functionality available to Apple Intelligence and Siri.
Each schema defines the requirements for intents, parameters, and results so people get a consistent experience across photo and video apps.
For example, a person can edit a photo on different apps that support the
schema with the same phrases.
Most schemas in this domain operate on assets that are already in your app’s library.
The exception is
, which adds new assets from an external source.
The following table maps example phrases that apply to each schema:
Photos intent schemas
Example phrases
“Add DSC_001.JPEG to the 2024 album.”
“Clean up.” or “Remove the clutter.”
“Create a new album named pics.”
“Save these to my photos.”
“Crop this photo.” or “Trim a bit off the borders.”
“Trash this album.” or “Get rid of vacation 2024.”
“Delete this photo.” or “Get rid of the three most recent photos.”
“Duplicate DSC_001.JPEG.” or “Make a copy of DSC_0123.JPEG.”
“Edit this photo.”
“Open the 2024 album.” or “Show me 2024.”
“Open DSC_0123.JPEG.” or “Play IMG_4567.MOV.”
“Share DSC_001.JPEG to the baby shared album.”
“Remove this photo from my shared album.”
“Apply the dramatic effect.” or “Remove the filter.”
“Rotate this photo clockwise.” or “Rotate this photo left.”
“Apply a warmth value of negative fifty.”
“Straighten this photo.” or “Make this photo straight.”
“Enhance this photo.” or “Make this photo better.”
“Rename vacation 2024 to trip.”
“Favorite this photo.” or “Hide this photo.”
Xcode generates a template implementation when you type
and select a schema from the suggestions list.
For more information about making your app’s actions available to Apple Intelligence and Siri, see

An intent schema that creates an album.

An intent schema that navigates to search results.
An intent schema that enhances a photo.
An intent schema that copies edits from the provided photo.
An intent schema that toggles depth effect for a photo.
An intent schema that configures the saturation for a photo.
Make your email app’s actions available to Apple Intelligence and Siri by adopting schemas for common email actions.
An intent schema that crops a photo.
An enum schema for a rotation direction parameter.
An intent schema that creates photos from the provided files.
An intent schema that removes the provided photos from the provided album.
An intent schema that deletes the provided albums.
Make your app’s actions available to Apple Intelligence and Siri by adopting schemas for in-app search and content access.
An intent schema that deletes the provided photos.
An intent schema that applies a filter to a photo.
An intent schema that straightens a photo.
An intent schema that posts the provided photos or files and optional comment to the provided shared album.
Make your calendar app’s actions available to Apple Intelligence and Siri by adopting schemas for common calendar actions.
An intent schema that opens the provided album.

abstract.text: Adopt photo schemas so people can edit and manage photos with Siri.
Make content and actions discoverable by Apple Intelligence and support system experiences like Siri, Spotlight, Shortcuts, and widgets.
Make your phone app’s actions available to Apple Intelligence and Siri by adopting schemas for calling actions.
An intent schema that sets the depth value of a photo.
An intent schema that removes distracting objects in a photo.
An intent schema that updates the provided person with new properties.
An intent schema that configures the warmth for a photo.
An intent schema that opens the app to a photo.
Identifies enum schemas in the photos domain.
An intent schema that opens a photo for editing.
An entity schema for an album.
An intent schema that adds the provided photos to the provided album.
Declare support for well-known actions and content by applying system-defined schemas to
your app intents, app entities, and app enumerations.
An entity schema for a recognized person.

Make your audio app’s actions available to Apple Intelligence and Siri by adopting schemas for common audio playback actions.

An intent schema that rotates a photo.

Identifies intent schemas in the photos domain.
